records.ety: [OE. binomen, p. p. of binimen to take away, AS. beniman; pref. be + niman to take. See Numb, a., and cf. Benim.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Benumbed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Benumbing.]

defs: To make torpid; to deprive of sensation or sensibility; to stupefy; as, a hand or foot benumbed by cold.
quotes: The creeping death benumbed her senses first.
